# Muk Buddy

> A patent-pending, reusable, 2-chamber wet/dry shop vac accessory designed and manufactured in the United States. Replaces hundreds of disposable shop vac bags for professional contractors, drywall crews, concrete finishers, and post-construction cleanup operations.

## Product

- **Name:** Muk Buddy
- **Category:** Reusable wet/dry shop vac accessory
- **Price:** $99 USD (one-time purchase)
- **Order page:** https://mukbuddy.com/ads
- **Checkout partner:** The Floor Lord (fulfillment + payment processing)
- **Patent status:** Patent Pending 2-chamber design
- **Manufactured in:** United States
- **Compatibility:** 12–20 gallon wet/dry vacs (Shop-Vac, Ridgid, Craftsman, Stanley, most DeWalt models). Does not fit 5-gallon units.

## Key features

- Patent-pending 2-chamber design separates wet pickup from dry pickup internally — no filter swap required between jobs.
- One reusable Muk Buddy replaces hundreds of disposable bags over its service life.
- Industrial-grade fabric — most working crews report 2+ years of daily use on a single Muk Buddy.
- Maintains stronger suction over time vs. disposable bags (no clogging from fines).
- Protects shop vac motor by preventing filter blockage and dust bypass.
- Designed for back-to-back jobs: rinse, drain, reload — no downtime.

## Who Muk Buddy is for

- Drywall contractors and finishers
- Concrete grinders and polishers
- Demolition, abatement, and remediation crews
- Auto detailers and auto body shops
- Janitorial and post-construction cleanup teams
- Restoration contractors (water, smoke, mold)
- Any working crew running a 12-20 gallon shop vac daily

## Problem Muk Buddy solves

Disposable shop vac bags clog with fines, tear under wet loads, lose suction within minutes, and require constant repurchase. A working crew can spend $80+/week per crew on disposable bags alone — not counting the lost time changing them mid-job and the motor wear from running clogged filters.

Muk Buddy is a single reusable accessory engineered to outlast hundreds of disposables, restore consistent suction, and pay for itself within weeks of daily contractor use.

## Trust signals

- Patent-pending design
- Made in the USA
- Built for daily contractor use
- 4.9 / 5 average customer rating
- Secure 256-bit SSL checkout via The Floor Lord (Visa, Mastercard, Amex)

## Blog posts

- [Why Disposable Shop Vac Bags Fail](https://mukbuddy.com/blog/why-disposable-shop-vac-bags-fail/)
- [Disposable vs Reusable Shop Vac Bags](https://mukbuddy.com/blog/disposable-vs-reusable-shop-vac-bags/)
- [Shop Vac Lost Suction: How to Fix It](https://mukbuddy.com/blog/shop-vac-lost-suction-how-to-fix/)
- [Why Your Shop Vac Clogs on Drywall Dust](https://mukbuddy.com/blog/why-shop-vac-clogs-on-drywall-dust/)
- [10 Reasons Reusable Beats Disposable](https://mukbuddy.com/blog/10-reasons-reusable-shop-vac-bag/)
- [The Real Cost of Running a Shop Vac Without a Bag](https://mukbuddy.com/blog/running-shop-vac-without-bag-cost/)
- [Is Shop Vac Downtime Costing Your Crew?](https://mukbuddy.com/blog/shop-vac-downtime-costing-your-crew/)
- [Do You Need a Bag in a Shop Vac?](https://mukbuddy.com/blog/do-you-need-a-bag-in-a-shop-vac/) — when a bag is required (fine dust) vs. optional (coarse/wet debris).
- [The 5 Best Shop Vac Bags](https://mukbuddy.com/blog/best-shop-vac-bags/) — ranked comparison of reusable vs. disposable options for fine-dust work.
- [Why Are Shop Vac Bags So Expensive?](https://mukbuddy.com/blog/why-are-shop-vac-bags-so-expensive/) — the razor-and-blades model and the ~$520/year cost per vac.

## Brand voice

Direct, contractor-to-contractor. No fluff. No DIY pandering. Honest about what disposable bags cost crews in money and downtime. Built for the truck, not the trade show.

## Contact

For fitment questions, bulk pricing for crews, or product support, use the contact form at https://mukbuddy.com/ads (replies within one business day).
